title: Europe must not ignore Egypt's persecuted Baha'is
summary: In a Euronews opinion piece, MEP Nikos Papandreou urges the EU to press Egypt to end discrimination against the Baha'i community. He argues Cairo should honour its constitutional commitment to freedom of belief by granting Baha'is equal legal rights.
imageAlt: Europe must not ignore Egypt's persecuted Baha'is
aiSummary: In a Euronews opinion piece, MEP Nikos Papandreou urges the EU to press Egypt to end discrimination against the Baha'i community. He argues Cairo should honour its constitutional commitment to freedom of belief by granting Baha'is equal legal rights.

fullArticle:
When my European Parliament colleagues and I visited Egypt, last week, we were delighted to strengthen our ties to a country with which we have a close and strategic partnership. Our connection is stronger than ever – underpinned by a €7.4 billion package, and underscored by last October’s first leaders’ summit – and I was glad to make the trip.

But one issue in Cairo left me troubled. The Baha’i minority religious community, some of whom I met during my visit, told me about the decades of discrimination and persecution they have endured from Egypt’s government and religious authorities.

The Baha’i Faith is an independent world religion, founded in the nineteenth century, whose central teaching is the oneness of humanity. Its several million followers live in virtually every country on e...
